response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software developer with strengths in web technologies, TypeScript, Node.js, and distributed systems, but the Principal Performance Architect role demands deep specialization in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, GPU architecture, and deep neural network optimization — none of which appear in his background. His performance work at Dunnhumby involved low-latency application-level tuning (async I/O, caching), which is categorically different from hardware-level performance analysis for silicon design. There is no evidence of hardware-software co-design, SIMD/MIMD architecture, or any DNN training/inference optimization work. The role's educational and experience requirements are technically met by tenure alone, but the domain mismatch is disqualifying.
strengths: Long career (~17 years) demonstrating sustained technical growth across diverse systems including embedded, distributed, and low-latency environments
Demonstrated performance engineering mindset at Dunnhumby: async I/O, fiber-based concurrency, high-throughput systems handling millions of requests per second in D lang

Breadth of systems experience including C/C++ at National Instruments and hardware-adjacent work (robotic arms, sound position server with multi-channel audio synthesis) shows comfort outside pure web stacks
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator hardware — the core technical requirements of the role
No background in deep learning workloads, neural network training or inference optimization, or any ML-adjacent performance analysis
Currently in an unexplained career gap since October 2025, and the most recent technical roles are in full-stack product engineering, moving further from hardware rather than toward it

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware modeling experience
No AI/DNN workload or GPU architecture background
Low-latency systems and async performance work at Dunnhumby
